title: Subsets
description: Given an integer array `nums` of distinct elements, return all possible subsets (the power set). The solution set must not contain duplicate subsets. Output is sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified form of each subset.

examples.explanation: All 8 subsets, sorted by JSON.stringify lex order (where ',' < ']').

hints: Har element ke liye 2 choice — include or skip.
DFS chala aur path push/pop kar.
Final list ko JSON.stringify se compare karke sort kar.

title: Combinations
description: Given two integers `n` and `k`, return all possible combinations of `k` numbers chosen from the range `1` to `n`. Each combination is a list of integers in increasing order. Output is sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified form of each combination.
hints: DFS me current start index se aage choose kar.
path.length === k pe push and return.
Sort at the end on stringified arrays.

title: Permutations
description: Given an array `nums` of distinct integers, return all possible permutations. The same index may not be reused within a single permutation. Output is sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified form of each permutation.
hints: Visited array ya boolean[] use kar to avoid reusing same index.
path.length === n pe push.
Final sort on stringified output.

title: Subsets With Target Sum
description: Given an array `nums` of distinct positive integers and an integer `target`, return all subsets of `nums` whose elements sum to exactly `target`. Each element may be used at most once. Output is sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified form of each subset.
hints: Subset DFS with running sum; push when sum === target.
Prune jab running sum > target ho jaye.
Lexicographic sort on JSON.stringify at the end.

title: Combination Sum
description: Given an array of distinct positive integers `prices` and an integer `budget`, return all unique combinations of `prices` whose elements sum to exactly `budget`. The same element may be chosen an unlimited number of times. Each combination must be listed in non-decreasing order, and the outer list is sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified form of each combination.
hints: Same index ko reuse karne do — recurse with start = i (not i+1).
Sort the prices first so combinations come out non-decreasing naturally.
Prune when remaining < 0.

title: Subsets II
description: Given an integer array `nums` that may contain duplicates, return all possible subsets (the power set) with no duplicate subsets in the result. Output is sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified form of each subset.
hints: Sort first so duplicates are adjacent.
Within same DFS level, skip nums[i] when nums[i] === nums[i-1] and i > start.
Final lexicographic sort on JSON.stringify.

title: Permutations II
description: Given an array `nums` that may contain duplicates, return all unique permutations in any internal order. The result must not contain duplicate permutations. Output is sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified form of each permutation.
hints: Sort first.
Used[] array; skip nums[i] if nums[i] === nums[i-1] && !used[i-1] (sibling already used).
Final lexicographic sort.

title: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number
description: Given a string `digits` containing digits from `'2'` to `'9'`, return all letter combinations that the number could represent, using the standard telephone keypad mapping (2→abc, 3→def, 4→ghi, 5→jkl, 6→mno, 7→pqrs, 8→tuv, 9→wxyz). If `digits` is empty, return `[]`. Output is sorted lexicographically.
hints: Map digit to its letter group.
DFS by index; concat letter and recurse.
Sort the final array lexicographically.

title: Combination Sum II
description: Given an integer array `candidates` (which may contain duplicates) and an integer `target`, return all unique combinations of `candidates` whose elements sum to exactly `target`. Each element from `candidates` may be used at most once in a combination. Each combination must be listed in non-decreasing order, and the outer list is sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified form of each combination.
hints: Sort to manage duplicates and prune.
Skip nums[i] === nums[i-1] when i > start.
Recurse with start = i + 1 (each used at most once).

title: Word Search
description: Given an `m x n` grid of characters `board` and a string `word`, return `true` if `word` can be constructed from letters of sequentially adjacent cells, where adjacent cells are horizontally or vertically neighboring. The same cell may not be used more than once. Otherwise return `false`.
hints: DFS from every cell that matches word[0].
Mark visited by overwriting with '#' temporarily; restore after.
Out-of-bounds and char mismatch → return false.

title: Generate Parentheses
description: Given an integer `n`, return all combinations of well-formed parentheses using exactly `n` pairs, each of total length `2n`. Output is sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified form of each string.
hints: Track open and close counts.
Open '(' allowed if open < n; close ')' allowed if close < open.
Push to result when length === 2n.

title: Palindrome Partitioning
description: Given a string `s`, partition `s` such that every substring of the partition is a palindrome, and return all possible palindrome partitionings. Each partitioning is a list of substrings, and the outer list is sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified form of each partitioning.
hints: DFS over start index; for each end > start, if s[start..end] is palindrome, recurse with start = end + 1.
Helper isPalindrome with two pointers.
Final lexicographic sort.

title: Restore IP Addresses
description: Given a string `s` containing only digits, return all possible valid IPv4 addresses that can be formed by inserting three dots into `s`. A valid address consists of four octets in the range `0` to `255`, with no leading zeros (so `"0"` is allowed but `"01"` is not). You may not reorder or remove any digits. Output is sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified form of each address.
hints: DFS with 4 segments to fill.
Each segment 1..3 chars; reject leading-zero (length>1 and starts with '0') and value > 255.
Stop early if remaining > 3 * remainingSegments or < remainingSegments.

title: Enumerate All Decodings
description: Given a string `s` containing only digits, where the encoding maps `A`=1 through `Z`=26, return all possible decoded strings. If `s` has a leading `'0'` or contains any group of digits that cannot be parsed into a valid letter, return `[]`. Output is sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified form of each decoded string.
hints: DFS by index; take 1 digit if '1'..'9'; take 2 digits if '10'..'26'.
Leading '0' or invalid group → dead end.
Final lexicographic sort.

title: Path Sum II
description: Given a binary tree `root` (provided as a level-order array with `null` marking absent children) and an integer `target`, return all root-to-leaf paths where the sum of the node values along the path equals `target`. Each path is returned as the list of node values from root to leaf, and the outer list is sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified form of each path.
hints: Build tree from level-order with nulls (BFS using a queue).
DFS down; at each leaf check sum.
Final sort on stringified arrays.

title: Letter Case Permutation
description: Given a string `s` of letters and digits, transform each letter to lowercase or uppercase to create new strings; digits remain unchanged. Return a list of all strings obtainable by toggling the case of the letters in any combination. Output is sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified form of each string.

examples.explanation: Sorted via localeCompare on JSON-stringified strings; locale order puts lowercase before uppercase.

constraints: s contains letters (a-z, A-Z) and digits
hints: DFS by index; for letter try both lower and upper; for digit only one branch.
Push complete string at i === n.
Final lexicographic sort.

title: N-Queens
description: Given an integer `n`, return all distinct solutions to the `n`-queens puzzle, where `n` queens are placed on an `n x n` chessboard so that no two queens attack each other (no two share a row, column, or diagonal). Each solution is a list of `n` strings representing the board, where `'Q'` marks a queen and `'.'` an empty cell. Output is sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified form of each solution.
hints: Place row by row.
Maintain three sets: cols, diag (r - c), anti (r + c).
Sort the final list of boards lexicographically.

title: Sudoku Solver
description: Given a partially filled `9 x 9` Sudoku `board` (a 2D array where `'.'` denotes an empty cell and `'1'`–`'9'` denote filled cells), fill the empty cells so that every row, every column, and each of the nine `3 x 3` sub-boxes contains the digits `1`–`9` exactly once. Return the completed board. Each puzzle is guaranteed to have a unique solution.

examples.output: the same grid filled per sudoku rules
examples.explanation: Standard sudoku rules: each row/col/3x3 box contains digits 1..9 exactly once.

constraints: Each test has exactly one valid solution
hints: Maintain three sets per row, col, box.
Find the next empty cell, try digits 1..9 that don't conflict, recurse, undo on dead end.
Box index = 3*Math.floor(r/3) + Math.floor(c/3).

title: Word Search II
description: Given an `m x n` grid of characters `board` and a list of strings `words`, return all words from `words` that can be constructed from letters of sequentially adjacent cells, where adjacent cells are horizontally or vertically neighboring. The same cell may not be used more than once within a single word. Output is sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified form of each word.
hints: For each word run word-search backtracking starting from any matching cell.
Mark visited with sentinel; restore on backtrack.
Push to result if found; final sort lexicographic.

title: Expression Add Operators
description: Given a string `num` containing only digits and an integer `target`, return all expressions formed by inserting the binary operators `'+'`, `'-'`, or `'*'` between the digits of `num` so that the expression evaluates to `target`. Operands may not contain leading zeros (except the single digit `'0'`), and standard operator precedence applies. Output is sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified form of each expression.
hints: DFS over splits + operator choice.
Track running value and last operand to handle '*' correctly: newValue = val - prev + prev*cur.
Skip operands with leading zero (length > 1 and starts with '0').
